preamble: Diphthongization of the close front unrounded vowel [i] to the diphthong [ia] through vowel breaking and glide insertion. This shift occurs in stressed syllables in Romance languages and represents a sporadic but recurring diphthongization pattern alongside the more common mid-vowel breaking.

examples.note: In Romance diphthongization patterns, stressed [i] develops into [ia] in certain phonological contexts, particularly in open syllables. French shows at least 5 vowel diphthongizations under stress accent.
Historical Spanish shows sporadic diphthongization of stressed vowels, with [i] occasionally becoming [ia] in certain contexts, though this is less regular than mid-vowel breaking.
Romanian maintains diphthong-hiatus contrasts and shows inheritance of Latin [i] developing into diphthongal realizations of iV sequences as [ia].
